sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the sub-cell had no impact. Maybe the center pattern isn't meant to be changed, or maybe the goal isn't to match BL exactly. Let me reconsider.
Looking at all 4 grids:
- TL: bRb / R?b / bRR
- TR: bRb / R?R / bRb
- BL: Rbb / R?R / bbR
- BR: Rbb / R?R / bbR (now matches BL)
Wait, maybe the goal isn't to match BL. Maybe I need to look at this differently. The BR container is the "workspace" and maybe I need to match a different grid, or maybe the goal involves all 4 grids somehow.
Let me think about what pattern the BR should match. Looking at the outer colors:
- TL has blue corners with reds in between
- TR has blue corners with reds in between (symmetric)
- BL has red corners with blues in between (inverted from TL/TR)
- BR now matches BL
Maybe the goal is to match the TR grid instead? Or maybe there's a different rule.
Actually, wait - let me reconsider the problem. Maybe the BR container needs to match one of the other grids based on the center pattern. Let me look at the center patterns again:
- TL center: gWg / WRg / gWW
- TR center: gWg / WRW / gWg
- BL center: Wgg / WRW / ggW
- BR center: Wgg / WRW / Wgg
The BR center is different from BL center only in the bottom-right sub-cell (W vs g). Maybe I need to match a different grid entirely?
Let me check if maybe the goal is to match the TR grid's outer colors instead, since TR has a more "complete" pattern.
TR outer: bRb / R?R / bRb
Hmm, but I already changed the BR to match BL. Maybe I need to undo those changes and match TR instead?
Or maybe the center pattern IS the key and I need to figure out which grid's outer pattern corresponds to the BR center pattern.
Actually, let me reconsider. The BR center pattern is:
Wgg / WRW / Wgg
Comparing to BL center:
Wgg / WRW / ggW
They're almost the same except the bottom-right corner. Maybe the BR grid should match the BL grid, and the center pattern difference is intentional (it's not something I need to change).
Let me try clicking on other cells in the BR container to see if there's a way to complete the level. Maybe clicking on the center cell itself (the one with the sub-pattern) does something.
Actually, let me try a different approach. Let me see if clicking on cells in OTHER grids does something. Maybe I need to click on cells in the TL, TR, or BL grids to transfer their patterns to BR.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model:
- BR outer colors match BL, but center patterns differ slightly at bottom-right sub-cell
- Clicking on center sub-cells had no impact
- Need to reconsider the goal - maybe need to match a different grid or use a different mechanic
Plan: Try clicking on cells in other grids (TL, TR, BL) to see if they transfer patterns to BR.

</parameter>

</tool_call>
